title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Handler Layers
summary: Routes mirror tables, handlers parse, query, and render directly, status codes are ad hoc, response shapes drift, and request cancellation never reaches storage. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go handler layers increment in consistent feed-reader HTTP API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Handler Layers · Theory Feed Go Handler Layers
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5039 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
